hypothesis: If we train ∆-learning XGBoost models to predict the correction from GFN1-xTB to DFT (PBE and HSE06) band gaps of MOFs using selected ST-135, RAC, and APRDF descriptors, then adding this correction to the xTB band gaps will yield significantly lower held-out MAE than direct XGBoost and fine-tuned MOFTransformer/CGCNN models because xTB captures most band-gap physics and ∆-learning focuses on systematic errors.
